questions.question: According to the 2025 prospective study, how does mindful eating reduce psychological distress?

options.text: Directly through relaxation and calming effects during meals
By reducing caloric intake and promoting weight loss
Indirectly — mindful eating leads to improved dietary practices, which then reduce distress
By increasing serotonin production during the act of eating

questions.explanation: The 2025 prospective study was the first to establish a directional pathway: mindful and intuitive eating leads to improved dietary practices, which in turn leads to reduced psychological distress. It works by changing actual food choices, not through direct psychological effects.

questions.question: On the hunger-fullness scale used in mindful eating, what is the ideal range to stop eating?

options.text: 1-2 (ravenous)
3-4 (clearly hungry)
8-10 (uncomfortably full)
6-7 (comfortably satisfied)

questions.explanation: The hunger-fullness scale runs from 1 (ravenous) to 10 (uncomfortably stuffed). The goal is to begin eating around 3-4 (clearly hungry) and stop around 6-7 (comfortably satisfied — could eat more but do not need to).

questions.question: What is a key difference between mindful eating and intuitive eating?

options.text: Mindful eating focuses on present-moment eating experience, while intuitive eating is a broader framework that also includes rejecting diet culture and respecting your body
Intuitive eating is based on scientific evidence, while mindful eating is not
Mindful eating requires strict portion control, while intuitive eating does not
They are identical concepts with different names

questions.explanation: Mindful eating focuses on present-moment awareness of eating — sensory qualities, hunger and satiety cues, and emotional triggers. Intuitive eating is a broader framework by Tribole and Resch that includes mindful eating principles plus rejecting diet culture, honoring hunger, making peace with food, respecting your body, and more.

questions.question: How can you distinguish physical hunger from emotional hunger?

options.text: Physical hunger always involves stomach pain, while emotional hunger does not
Physical hunger comes on gradually and can be satisfied by many foods; emotional hunger comes on suddenly and craves specific comfort foods
Emotional hunger only occurs at night, while physical hunger occurs throughout the day
There is no reliable way to distinguish between them

questions.explanation: Physical hunger comes on gradually, can be satisfied by many different foods, stops when you are full, and does not produce guilt. Emotional hunger comes on suddenly, craves specific comfort foods, persists even when physically full, and often triggers guilt or shame afterward.

questions.question: In the four-week mindful eating protocol, what is the focus of Week 1?

options.text: Eliminating all snacking between meals
Tracking hunger and fullness on the 1-10 scale at every meal
Eating one meal per day without screens or distractions
Simply noticing your current eating patterns without making any changes

questions.explanation: Week 1 of the protocol is about awareness without change. You simply notice your current eating patterns — asking before each meal: Am I physically hungry? What am I feeling emotionally? Where am I eating? Am I distracted? No right or wrong answers, just observation.

title: Mindful Eating for Mental Health - Quiz
